qualifiedClaim.summary: Chio ships a cryptographically signed, fail-closed governance and evidence control plane with bounded delegated-authority semantics, bounded hosted/auth profiles, explicit provenance classes, signed receipts and checkpoints, and explicit local or leader-local operational contracts for trust-control, budgets, and review surfaces.
constraints: This is the primary ship-facing release boundary.

The bounded release excludes consensus-grade HA, distributed-linearizable budget authority, public transparency-log semantics, and authenticated recursive delegation ancestry beyond the preserved presented chain.
Stronger cross-protocol, universal-control-plane, and comptroller-capable gates remain optional repo-local addenda rather than the ship-facing release claim.

nonQualifiedClaims: theorem-prover completion for every protocol claim
authenticated recursive delegation ancestry beyond the preserved presented chain
verifier-backed runtime assurance as the sole admission boundary
consensus-grade HA or distributed-linearizable budget truth
public transparency-log or strong non-repudiation semantics

gateConditions.summary: Ship-facing docs align on one bounded Chio claim and explicit non-claims.
Delegated-authority language matches the hot path actually shipped.
One bounded hosted/auth profile is published and compatibility paths are explicitly demoted.
Governed provenance surfaces distinguish asserted, observed, and verified truth or are explicitly narrowed.
The bounded operational profile records which surfaces are local-only, leader-local, informational-only, or compatibility-only.
The Track A P0 checklist maps blockers to evidence and sign-off expectations.
